---
title: "ev — ExaVerif"
subtitle: "Exhaustive Verification for RISC-V Custom Instructions"
date: last-modified
metadata-files:
  - _include/format.html.yml
abstract: |
  ev (ExaVerif) is an open-source (Apache 2.0) exhaustive verification CLI for
  RISC-V custom instruction extensions.  Given a YAML specification of
  instruction fields and constraints, ev generates every valid encoding,
  evaluates each constraint, and reports pass/fail -- deterministically, with
  no random sampling.
---

## Overview

RISC-V custom instruction extensions are typically verified with constrained-random
tools (riscv-dv) or formal property checkers (OneSpin). Both approaches leave
coverage gaps: random simulation cannot prove absence of invalid encodings, and
formal tools require significant expertise to set up and maintain.

ev takes a different approach. Rather than sampling the encoding space or proving
properties about it, ev exhaustively enumerates every valid combination of
instruction fields, evaluates all constraints, and reports the result for every
single encoding. The answer is complete, deterministic, and reproducible.

## Recent Documents

{{< include _include/_updated_docs_list.qmd >}}

<br />

## How It Works

A single YAML file describes the instruction encoding space:

```yaml
target: simple_alu
fields:
  op_a:    { range: [0, 15] }
  op_b:    { range: [0, 15] }
  op_code: { values: [0, 1, 2, 3] }
constraints:
  - type: eq
    field: op_a
    value: 0
projector: { type: sum }
```

ev parses this into a domain-agnostic internal representation (VerificationSpec),
computes the cartesian product of all field domains (with an overflow guard at
10 million combinations), evaluates every point against the constraint set, and
projects each result through one or more projector functions (sum, parity, identity).

Capability-trait architecture (same pattern as NeXus):

| Extension Point | How |
|:---|:---|
| New constraint type | `ConstraintRegistry::register("name", builder)` |
| New projector | `ProjectorRegistry::register("name", builder)` |
| New input format | Implement `FormatCapable` trait |
| New output format | Implement `ReporterCapable` trait |

## Scale

256 fields, 10 million constraint evaluations, sub-second.

A real-world target -- the CVA6 core's CV-X-IF coprocessor interface --
generates 33,554,432 possible operand combinations across four custom opcode
spaces. ev evaluates all of them in 32 seconds on a single Apple M1 Max core,
identifying 196,608 valid encodings. Every valid encoding was independently
confirmed through Spike simulation.

## Independent Cross-Verification

ev independently validates against hand-written RISC-V assembly from the
SSCCS Proof-of-Concept. The same set of constraints is expressed twice --
once as Rust code in ev, once as handwritten RISC-V assembly -- and both paths
must produce identical results:

```text
narrow:   even ∧ range_0_10  →  2,REJECT,REJECT,10,REJECT  ✓
broad:    no constraints     →  2,3,5,10,12                ✓
sum3d_a:  (2,1,0)            →  3                          ✓
sum3d_b:  (1,2,3)            →  6                          ✓
parity:   {2,3}              →  0,1                        ✓
```

Nineteen such golden anchors are auto-verified by `check_golden_anchors.py`;
any change to the assembly forces a cross-substrate consistency check.

## Potential

Exhaustive verification for custom instructions is a natural fit for domain-specific
accelerators, cryptographic instruction extensions, and any scenario where correctness
of every encoding matters. ev's trait-based extension model makes it straightforward
to add new constraint types, projection functions, and output formats without modifying
the core pipeline.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Full CI pipeline
./run.sh

# Cross-verify against SSCCS PoC golden anchors
./run.sh --demo

# Verify an instruction specification
ev verify --target tests/fixtures/sample.xif.yaml

# Verify with JSON output
ev verify --target tests/fixtures/sample.xif.yaml --json

# Simulate all valid encodings on Spike
EV_SIM_BACKEND=spike ev simulate --target tests/fixtures/sample.xif.yaml
```

## Ecosystem

ev is part of the [SSCCS Foundation](https://ssccs.org) ecosystem.

- [SSCCS PoC](https://github.com/ssccsorg/ssccs): Core field composition engine, Scheme abstraction layer
- [neXus](https://docs.ssccs.org/projects/nexus/): A swarm computing runtime fabric for autonomous execution.


## License

Apache 2.0. Copyright 2026 [SSCCS Foundation](https://ssccs.org).
